name: Baron Samedi
shortDescription: Baron Samedi is a prominent loa (spirit) in Haitian Vodou, known as the master of the dead. He is the leader of the Guédé family of spirits and is associated with death, resurrection, and cemeteries.
description: Baron Samedi is one of the most well-known and powerful loa within Haitian Vodou. He stands at the crossroads between the living and the dead, greeting souls as they enter the afterlife and ensuring they are properly interred. As master of the graveyard, he is also responsible for ensuring that the dead stay in their graves unless called forth for a specific purpose. He is often depicted as a skeleton or a man dressed in formal attire, including a top hat, dark glasses (often with one lens missing), and a cotton plug in his nostrils, resembling the appearance of a corpse prepared for burial. Baron Samedi is known for his crude humor, love of rum and tobacco, and his penchant for causing mischief. Despite his association with death, he is also a symbol of life, resurrection, and healing, as he has the power to accept or deny entrance to the afterlife. He is also called upon for protection and divination. His role is complex, encompassing both the fearsome aspects of death and the vital connection to ancestors. He is a key figure in Vodou practices, reminding followers of the cyclical nature of life and death and the importance of respecting the ancestral spirits.

sources.title: Divine Horsemen: The Living Gods of Haiti
sources.author: Maya Deren

sources.title: Vodou in Haitian Culture and Life
sources.author: Claudine Michel
